must_detect.matched_text:
Parties: Azure Banyan Holdings Limited (HKEX: 83Z1) as purchaser; Coral Vista Technology Limited as target; and Lattice Shore Fund SPC – Cell A as co-investor.
Transaction: Proposed acquisition of 55% of the issued shares via a share purchase agreement; this non-binding term sheet is subject to HKEX Listing Rules Chapter 14/14A.
Consideration: To be determined within a valuation range referencing a 30-trading-day VWAP; no price-sensitive figures are set out and no profit forecasts are included.
Regulatory: Any announcement obligations under Part XIVA of the Securities and Futures Ordinance will be assessed; the parties confirm no MNPI is being provided beyond information already released on the HKEX news website on 2025-12-10.
Approvals: SFC consent (if required), independent shareholders’ approval, and LPAC confirmation for Lattice Shore Fund SPC; long-stop date six months from signing.
Data protection: Draft Data Processing Addendum (DPA) Schedule 2 (Retention and Minimisation) forms part of the diligence pack and sets policy-only retention periods without triggering collection expansion.
Cross-border transfer approval: Data Export Approval REQ-AX17 was granted on 2026-01-15 for limited payroll and directory fields to be transferred from Hong Kong to a service provider in Singapore under intra-group model clauses, with transfer logs archived.
Consent/DSAR status: There are no outstanding consent withdrawals, no open data subject access requests, and no erasure requests pending as at the date of this term sheet.
Onboarding forms: Purchaser and fund will use generic onboarding/KYC forms capturing role-based emails and business contacts only; no HKID numbers or residential addresses will be collected at this stage.
Employment: No changes to employee terms or benefit plans until completion; any transfer of employee records will be limited to field subsets specified in DPA Schedule 3.
Information sharing: Virtual data room access is restricted to necessary folders; any “inside information” tags are policy labels and do not indicate undisclosed price-sensitive facts.
